name: AI Native CMS
one_liner: Open-source CLI that migrates WordPress sites to clean Astro + MDX codebases — the wedge for an AI-native CMS where content lives as files an AI agent can edit cleanly.

stack.backend: n/a (CLI — emits Astro projects for downstream Cloudflare Pages / Vercel hosting)
stack.dependencies: fast-xml-parser, @wordpress/block-serialization-default-parser, zod, yaml, sharp, simple-git, vitest
stack.architecture: source-adapter (WXR | REST) → versioned Zod IR → emitter (Astro + MDX)

demo_pattern: User runs `npx wp-to-astro@latest migrate <wxr-or-url> --out <dir>` → gets a clean Astro repo with content as MDX files → opens in Claude Code or Cursor → vibe-codes edits across all posts at once.
notes: OSS CLI shipped to npm v0.6.1 (2026-05-30) and GitHub public. 6 passes built end-to-end in 2 sessions: WXR adapter → block mapping → image pipeline → REST adapter → SEO metadata → redirects + verify. v0.6.0 had a `slug` reserved-field bug surfaced by real-world test against Docker WP; v0.6.1 patched. v0.7 punch list: MDX-compat sanitization (br, comments, stray <), --permalink flag for WXR, non-Latin slug fallback, verify --build flag. Now in validation phase per Hamzaish discipline — must do 5 WP-refugee conversations before further build.

validation.state: 5 real WP-refugee migrations + 3 'I'd pay $X' signals (source: validation-sprint notes — the recorded debt)

traction.state: 100 GitHub stars OR 10 unsolicited paid expressions of interest (source: GitHub + inbound — the product's own recorded gate)
